title: lotus corniculatus subsp. japonicus
intro: Lotus corniculatus subsp. japonicus, also known as Lotus Japonicus, is a subspecies of perennial herb native to East Asia, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and distinctive leaf shape. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Lotus Japonicus include reducing symptoms of menopause, alleviating menstrual cramps, and supporting cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as dried flower and root powder, tea, and tincture, for use in traditional medicine and as a dietary supplement.
